title: write_firrtl keeps adding underscores
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.55+8 (git sha1 02323295b, sccache clang++ 20.1.6 -fPIC -O3 -O1 -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fno-optimize-sibling-calls -fsanitize=address,undefined)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps
```
read_verilog <<EOT
module top(input wire a, b, output wire c);
assign c = a + b;
endmodule
EOT

prep
write_firrtl a.firrtl
write_firrtl b.firrtl
write_firrtl c.firrtl
```
### Expected Behavior

All firrtl files are the same

### Actual Behavior

Each time we call `write_firrtl` it appends an extra underscore to identifiers.

a.firrtl starts
```
circuit top: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
  module top: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
    input a: UInt<1> @[<<EOT:1.23-1.24]
```

b.firrtl starts
```
circuit top_: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
  module top_: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
    input a_: UInt<1> @[<<EOT:1.23-1.24]
```

c.firrtl starts
```
circuit top__: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
  module top__: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
    input a__: UInt<1> @[<<EOT:1.23-1.24]
```

I guess no one uses this pass but might as well fix it.
code_blocks:
read_verilog <<EOT
module top(input wire a, b, output wire c);
assign c = a + b;
endmodule
EOT

prep
write_firrtl a.firrtl
write_firrtl b.firrtl
write_firrtl c.firrtl

circuit top: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
  module top: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
    input a: UInt<1> @[<<EOT:1.23-1.24]

circuit top_: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
  module top_: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
    input a_: UInt<1> @[<<EOT:1.23-1.24]

circuit top__: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
  module top__: @[<<EOT:1.1-3.10]
    input a__: UInt<1> @[<<EOT:1.23-1.24]
